_schema.description: Nutrifence nutrition report contract. The DEFAULT contract is grounded in the official Federal Ministry of Health Food-Based Dietary Guidelines for Nigeria (FBDG, WHO-endorsed, 2006). Clinical condition contracts (cardiovascular, diabetes, obesity) are extracted directly from the FBDG. User-submitted nutritionist reports are parsed into a USER_SUBMITTED contract and layered on top of the FBDG default.

authority.DEFAULT: Federal Ministry of Health Nigeria + WHO — Food-Based Dietary Guidelines for Nigeria (2006)
authority.NUTRITIONIST_PARSED: Professional nutritionist document uploaded by the user or clinic
authority.USER_SUBMITTED: Self-reported by the user via the app

priorityOrder: NUTRITIONIST_PARSED — highest authority. Enforced strictly. Overrides DEFAULT where they conflict.
USER_SUBMITTED — medium authority. Enforced but flagged as AI-extracted.
DEFAULT (FBDG) — always active as baseline. Never overridden completely — FBDG constraints apply even when a nutritionist report is present.
_schema.howContractsStack: The pipeline always starts with DEFAULT (FBDG). If a user uploads a nutritionist report, USER_SUBMITTED or NUTRITIONIST_PARSED is merged on top. Conflicts are resolved in favour of the higher-authority contract. The FBDG condition-specific tables (cardiovascular, diabetes, obesity) are automatically activated when matching conditions are present in the active contract — regardless of contract type.

llmInputContract.injectionNote: Serialize the active contract's llmInstructions + constraints into the Groq system prompt using the pipelineIntegration.promptInjectionTemplate. Always include the FBDG baseline. If a user contract is active, append its instructions after the FBDG instructions.

DEFAULT.source: Federal Ministry of Health Nigeria — Food-Based Dietary Guidelines for Nigeria (WHO, 2006)
DEFAULT.rawText: No single food by itself (except breast-milk) provides all the nutrients in the right amounts that will promote growth and maintain life. Eat a variety of foods. Increase consumption of fruits, vegetables, grains and legumes. Increase consumption of poultry and fish. Use more fats from vegetable sources. Decrease consumption of foods high in animal fat. Decrease consumption of sugar and foods high in sugar content. Decrease consumption of salt and foods high in salt content. Drink water as much as possible daily.

generalPrinciples: Eat a variety of foods at every meal — no single food provides all nutrients
Eat adequate diet prepared from available food items at least 3 times daily
Total food intake should reflect physical activity level
excludedIngredients: organ meats in excess (liver, kidney, brain/kwanya, intestine)
fatty cuts (hump/tozo, tongue, cowtail, ponmo/skin, bacon, sausage, hamburger)
pastries (puff-puff, doughnut, chin-chin, meat pie, sausage rolls, cakes)
reduceIntake: alcohol — if consumed, only in moderation

macroGuards.portionNotes: Total food intake adjusted to physical activity level. Avoid second helpings.

mealTimingNotes: Drink water as much as possible throughout the day
constraints.physicalActivity: Adults should undertake some form of exercise as recommended by their doctor. At minimum 20 minutes, 3 times per week.

conditionTables._note: These tables are extracted verbatim from the FBDG. They are activated automatically when a matching condition is present in the user's profile.

reduceIntake: fatty meat/fish (hump/tozo, tongue, cowtail, ponmo, bacon, sausage, hamburger, mackerel)
oils rich in saturated fats (palm oil, coconut oil, palm kernel oil)
refined processed foods (semovita, cornvita, baking flour, spaghetti, macaroni, noodles)
avoid: pastries (meat pie, sausage rolls, chocolate, cookies, cakes)
refined sugar and foods with added sugar
salty foods (corned beef, sardine, bullion cubes, MSG, salted fish/meat)
adding salt to cooked food at the table
cookingNotes: Cook or boil food instead of frying
If hypertensive: reduce salt intake to the barest minimum
Exercise: strolling, brisk walking, jogging, skipping, swimming — not less than 20 minutes, 3 times a week

diabetes.note: Diabetes is often associated with obesity and hypertension. Apply cardiovascular guidelines simultaneously.

starchyFoods: whole grain (maize, millet, guinea corn, acha)
roots/tubers (water yam, Irish potatoes, bitter yam/esuru)
sugaryFoods: fresh diluted fruit juices (especially lemon/lime juice)
vegetables: green leafy vegetables with every meal
fruits: one or two portions of fruits in season (oranges, guava, mangoes)

reduceIntake: highly refined flour products (semolina, cassava flour)
oils rich in saturated fats (palm oil, coconut oil, palm kernel oil)
starchy thickeners in soups (cocoyam, yam flour, starch, garri)
salty foods (corned beef, sardine, bullion cubes/MSG)
avoid: fatty meat/fish (hump/tozo, tongue, cowtail, intestine, ponmo, bacon, sausage, hamburger, mackerel)
fats from animal sources (lard, butter, chicken fat, turkey fat)
cookingNotes: Cook or boil food instead of frying
Reduce salt intake, especially if hypertensive
Can have carbonated drink (soda water) but if hypertensive, consult doctor
Always take diabetes medication as prescribed
Exercise: brisk walking, swimming, cycling, dancing — at least 20 minutes, 3 times a week (under medical supervision)

obesity_weight_loss.generalNote: Maintain body weight appropriate for age and size by controlling total food intake. Water intake may be increased to control appetite. Reduce second helpings. Eat slowly.

sugaryFoods: fresh unsweetened diluted fruit juice (especially lemon/lime)
fruits: one or two portions of fruits daily (oranges, grapefruits, any fruits in season)

reduceIntake: refined cereals and tubers (wheat flour, cassava flour/elubo, yam flour)
fatty meat/fish (hump/tozo, tongue, cowtail, ponmo, bacon, sausage, hamburger, mackerel)
oils rich in saturated fats (palm oil, coconut oil, palm kernel oil)
avoid: organ meats (liver, kidney, brain/kwanya) — especially if blood cholesterol is high
fats from animal sources (chicken fat, turkey fat, lards, butter)
cookingNotes: Cook and boil food instead of frying
Exercise regularly: at least 20 minutes, 3 times per week — brisk walking, swimming, climbing stairs, skipping, jogging
Eat while sitting — avoid eating on the run
Eat slowly to allow satiety signals to reach the brain
Do not skip meals — weight recycling and meal skipping are not desirable

llmInstructions: You are applying the official Federal Ministry of Health Nigeria Food-Based Dietary Guidelines (FBDG, WHO 2006) as the baseline nutrition standard for this Nigerian user.
Always recommend a variety of foods — no single food is sufficient on its own.
Prioritise: lean fish (tilapia, stockfish), skinless poultry, legumes (beans, cowpeas, soyabeans), green leafy vegetables, seasonal fruits, whole grains and tubers.
Always reduce: excess salt, bullion cubes, MSG, refined sugar, sugary drinks, animal fat, highly processed foods.

Always avoid recommending: organ meats in excess, fatty cuts (ponmo, sausage, bacon, hamburger), full cream dairy, deep fried foods (puff-puff, chin-chin, doughnuts), pastries (meat pie, sausage roll, cakes), sweetened carbonated drinks, tobacco.
Preferred cooking methods: boiling, grilling, steaming, stir-frying. Never recommend deep frying as a preparation method.
Palm oil is acceptable in small, measured amounts (unbleached). Do not present palm oil as a health food or recommend it freely — FBDG classifies it as a saturated fat to reduce.

If the user has cardiovascular disease or hypertension: activate cardiovascular_hypertension condition table. Reduce salt to the barest minimum. Avoid all saturated fats, fried foods, and salty condiments.

If the user has diabetes: activate diabetes condition table. Prioritise whole grains, unripe plantain, bitter leaf, garden egg. Avoid all sugary foods, ripe plantain, banana, sweet potato, refined flour products, and soft drinks entirely.

If the user has obesity or weight_loss goal: activate obesity_weight_loss condition table. Increase vegetables and fibre, reduce refined carbohydrates, avoid all pastries and sugary drinks, emphasise portion control and boiling over frying.
When multiple conditions are present (e.g. diabetes + hypertension): apply both condition tables simultaneously. The more restrictive rule always wins.
For local Nigerian canteens: swallow (fist size) + vegetable soup is FBDG-compliant at lunch. Recommend requesting soup without excess stock cubes.
For pepper soup venues: fish pepper soup and chicken pepper soup are FBDG-compliant choices. Flag if broth may contain excess MSG or salt.
For fast food chains: grilled chicken without skin is the safest option. Fried chicken, chips, and soft drinks all conflict with FBDG.
Always ground recommendations in Nigerian food culture and name Nigerian dishes correctly.

NUTRITIONIST_PARSED._comment: Populated when a professional nutritionist report is uploaded. Example below is from Ask Damz — Dr Oluwadamilola (Silver Package, Weight Loss Plan). Replace with actual extracted values per user.
NUTRITIONIST_PARSED.source: Ask Damz — Dr Oluwadamilola (Silver Package, Weight Loss Plan)

fbdgAlignment.note: This plan is broadly aligned with FBDG obesity/weight_loss guidelines. Where they conflict, this nutritionist plan takes precedence. FBDG baseline still applies for any food not addressed by this plan.
overrides: Dinner rule (no protein + carbs) is stricter than FBDG — enforced from this contract
Preferred oils (avocado, coconut, extra virgin olive oil) override FBDG's preferred oils for this user
Sweeteners (stevia, monk fruit only) override FBDG's general sugar-reduction guidance

NUTRITIONIST_PARSED.rawText: Each meal should fit into one plate. Half the plate should be vegetables, a quarter of the plate should be proteins and the rest of the plate should be carbohydrates and fats. You must drink at least 1/1.5 litres of water every day. No deep greasy food and No refined sugar in any form whether brown or white in this process. Do not mix proteins and carbs for dinner ever. Snacks could be fruits, protein and vegetables. Each snack should fit into one palm. The recommended cooking oils are avocado oil, coconut oil, extra virgin olive oil, use a tablespoon or teaspoon for measure. Spaghetti pack should be divided into four portions and half a portion should be used. Portion control is very important.

patientProfile.activityNotes: 30-45 minutes physical activity, 3-4 days per week.

macroGuards.portionNotes: Half plate vegetables, quarter plate protein, quarter plate carbs+fats. Snack fits in one palm. Swallow: fist size. Spaghetti: one eighth of standard pack.

portionRules: Half plate must be vegetables at every main meal
Spaghetti: one eighth of a standard pack per serving
Snack must fit in one palm
Skinless chicken: 2 medium pieces per meal
dinnerRules: STRICT: Never mix protein and carbohydrates at dinner
Acceptable dinners: pepper soup, grilled chicken with salad, egg salad, vegetable soup without swallow, avocado salad, stir fry without rice or swallow
No rice, yam, plantain, potato, pasta, or any swallow at dinner
snackRules: Snacks limited to fruits, protein, vegetables
Each snack must fit in one palm
Acceptable snacks: apple, carrot, pineapple, watermelon, cucumber, grape, avocado, 2 boiled eggs
contraindications: No refined sugar in any form at any meal
No deep fried or greasy food
Never mix protein and carbs at dinner

cheatDayPolicy.rule: Cheat moderately. Notify nutritionist before cheating.

llmInstructions: This user is on a supervised Nigerian weight loss plan (Ask Damz, Dr Oluwadamilola). Apply FBDG obesity guidelines as baseline PLUS these additional rules.
DINNER IS STRICTLY PROTEIN AND VEGETABLES ONLY — never recommend rice, yam, plantain, swallow, pasta, or any carbohydrate at dinner.
Never recommend deep fried food, refined sugar, or sugary drinks.
Preferred proteins: skinless chicken (grilled/boiled), fish (grilled/boiled/pepper soup), lean beef, turkey, eggs.
Portion sizes are mandatory: 2 medium pieces protein per meal, half plate vegetables.
For pepper soup venues: fish pepper soup and chicken pepper soup are ideal dinner choices — fully compliant.
For fast food chains: grilled chicken only. Reject fried chicken, chips, and all drinks except water.
For local canteens: swallow + vegetable soup is acceptable at LUNCH only. Never at dinner.
Cheat day is Saturday — acknowledge flexibility but still avoid deep fried and refined sugar.
Cooking oils: avocado oil, coconut oil, extra virgin olive oil in measured amounts only.

USER_SUBMITTED_TEMPLATE._comment: Shell populated when a user uploads their own nutritionist report. Parsed by LLM using userReportParsingPrompt. The fbdgAlignment block must always be filled — it records which FBDG rules this report overrides vs aligns with.

constraints.confidenceNote: User-submitted report — AI extracted. Verify critical constraints before clinical use. FBDG baseline remains active.

userReportParsingPrompt._comment: Send this to the LLM when a user uploads their own nutritionist report. Replace {{REPORT_TEXT}} with the extracted document text. The LLM returns a populated USER_SUBMITTED contract.
userReportParsingPrompt.systemPrompt:
You are a clinical nutrition data extractor for a Nigerian health app.
Your job is to read a nutritionist report or meal plan and extract structured data into a precise JSON format.

EXTRACTION RULES:
1. Extract what is explicitly stated AND infer conditions from goal language.
2. Use the goal-to-condition mapping table below — this is mandatory, not optional.
3. Unknown values: null for strings/numbers, [] for arrays.
4. The Nigerian FBDG (Federal Ministry of Health, WHO 2006) is always the baseline.
   Your job is only to extract what this specific report ADDS or OVERRIDES on top of that baseline.

GOAL-TO-CONDITION MAPPING (always apply this):
"lose weight" | "weight loss" | "slimming" | "slim down" | "fat loss" | "reduce weight" | "weight management" | "obese" | "overweight"
  -> conditions: ["weight_loss"], goal: "weight_loss"

"diabetes" | "diabetic" | "blood sugar" | "sugar level" | "glucose" | "t2dm" | "type 2"
  -> conditions: ["diabetes"], goal: "diabetes_management"

"blood pressure" | "hypertension" | "hbp" | "high bp" | "pressure"
  -> conditions: ["hypertension"], goal: "hypertension_management"

"heart" | "cardiovascular" | "cholesterol" | "cardiac"
  -> conditions: ["cardiovascular"], goal: "cardiovascular_health"

"muscle" | "build muscle" | "gain muscle" | "bulk"
  -> conditions: [], goal: "muscle_gain"

"general wellness" | "healthy eating" | "balanced diet" | "eat healthy"
  -> conditions: [], goal: "general_wellness"

If the document contains multiple goal signals, include all matching conditions.
For example "diabetic patient trying to lose weight" -> conditions: ["diabetes", "weight_loss"]
userReportParsingPrompt.userPrompt:
Read the following nutritionist report carefully.

STEP 1 — Before writing any JSON, identify:
a) What is the primary goal of this plan? (weight loss / diabetes management / etc.)
b) What conditions or health goals are mentioned, implied, or addressed?
c) Map each to the normalized condition labels from the mapping table.

STEP 2 — Extract all information into this exact JSON structure.
Fill every field you can find evidence for. Leave fields null or [] if genuinely not mentioned.
Generate llmInstructions: 5-10 imperative sentences the AI should follow when filtering
restaurant meals for this user. Base them strictly on what the nutritionist wrote.
Fill fbdgAlignment.overrides with any rules in this report that differ from standard FBDG guidance.

Return ONLY the JSON — no markdown, no explanation, no preamble.

Target structure:
{
  "reportId": "user_report_{{timestamp}}",
  "contractType": "USER_SUBMITTED",
  "source": "user_uploaded",
  "createdAt": "{{current ISO timestamp}}",
  "fbdgAlignment": {
    "note": "brief sentence on how this plan relates to FBDG",
    "overrides": []
  },
  "rawText": "{{first 500 chars of document}}",
  "patientProfile": {
    "age": null,
    "sex": null,
    "weightKg": null,
    "heightCm": null,
    "activityLevel": null,
    "goal": null,
    "activityNotes": null
  },
  "constraints": {
    "conditions": [],
    "excludedIngredients": [],
    "allergensAvoid": [],
    "preferredFoods": [],
    "preferredSweeteners": [],
    "preferredCookingOils": [],
    "macroGuards": {
      "maxSodiumMg": null,
      "maxSugarG": null,
      "maxSatFatG": null,
      "minFiberG": null,
      "targetProteinG": null,
      "maxCaloriesPerMeal": null,
      "portionNotes": null
    },
    "portionRules": [],
    "mealTimingNotes": [],
    "dinnerRules": [],
    "breakfastOptions": [],
    "snackRules": [],
    "cookingMethodPreference": [],
    "cookingMethodAvoid": [],
    "contraindications": [],
    "cheatDayPolicy": null,
    "confidenceNote": "User-submitted report — AI extracted. FBDG baseline remains active."
  },
  "llmInstructions": []
}

Nutritionist report text:
{{REPORT_TEXT}}

pipelineIntegration._comment: How nutrifence_pipeline.js injects the active contract into the Groq explainWithGroq() call.
pipelineIntegration.activeContractResolution: Load DEFAULT (FBDG) always. If user has a NUTRITIONIST_PARSED or USER_SUBMITTED contract, merge it on top. Activate matching FBDG conditionTables for any conditions present across both contracts.
